Classical thermodynamics: entropy and the arrow of time
Electrical engineering: signal processing and the Nyquist theorem

Immunology: autoimmune diseases — lupus, multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis (mechanisms)
Neuroscience: dopamine circuits and reward prediction error

Organic chemistry: peptide bond formation and Ramachandran plots
Physical chemistry: molecular orbital theory vs valence bond
reaction kinetics and transition state theory
Biochemistry: ATP synthesis via chemiosmosis (Mitchell's chemiosmotic hypothesis)
Astrochemistry: origin of life chemistry (Miller-Urey, RNA world)

Ancient philosophy: Platonic forms and the allegory of the cave
Stoicism — Epictetus' Enchiridion, Marcus Aurelius' Meditations, Seneca's Letters
Modern philosophy: Hume's problem of induction and bundle theory of self
Kant's categorical imperative and the synthetic a priori
Ethics: utilitarianism — Bentham, Mill, and preference utilitarianism (Singer)
Kantian deontology and perfect vs imperfect duties
Philosophy of mind: Turing test and the imitation game
Political philosophy: Hobbes' Leviathan and the social contract
Locke on property and the right of revolution
Nietzsche's will to power and master-slave morality

Ancient history: the Bronze Age collapse (~1200 BCE) — causes and debates
Han dynasty bureaucracy and the civil service exam
the Library of Alexandria — myth vs reality
Medieval history: Black Death societal impact — labor, church, and flagellants
the Islamic Golden Age — Al-Kindi, Ibn Rushd, Al-Khwarizmi
Byzantine resilience — Greek fire, themata system
the Mongol Pax and knowledge transfer
Early modern history: the Scientific Revolution as a social phenomenon
the printing press and information revolution
colonial knowledge transfer and botanical imperialism
Modern history: the Second Industrial Revolution (electricity, chemicals, steel)
WWI chemical warfare — chlorine, phosgene, mustard gas
the Cold War science race — ICBM, Sputnik, Apollo
History of science: the calculus priority dispute (Newton vs Leibniz)
the discovery of oxygen (Scheele, Priestley, Lavoisier)
resistance to germ theory (Semmelweis, Koch, Pasteur)

Language universals: Chomsky's universal grammar and poverty of the stimulus
the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis — strong vs weak versions
Historical linguistics: the comparative method and tree models vs wave models
Phonology & phonetics: vocal tract acoustics and formant frequencies
Semiotics: the pragmatics of meaning — Grice's maxims
Cognitive linguistics: the lexical gap and untranslatable words

Oceanography: thermohaline circulation and the ocean conveyor belt
Climatology: permafrost feedback loops and methane release
tipping points in the climate system

Mass extinctions: the Permian-Triassic extinction — causes and selectivity
the K-Pg impact event and Chicxulub
the Cambrian explosion — triggers and body plan diversification
Human evolution: Homo naledi and non-linear human evolution
Denisovan introgression and its modern legacy
the cognitive revolution — language, art, symbolic thought
the aquatic ape hypothesis and other controversies
Fossil record: the Burgess Shale organisms and Cambrian ecology
feathered dinosaurs and the bird transition
Deep time: Snowball Earth events — evidence and escape mechanism
the Ediacaran biota — pre-Cambrian complex life

World literature: Dostoevsky — Crime and Punishment, the Grand Inquisitor (Brothers Karamazov)
Borges — labyrinths, the library of Babel, and time
García Márquez and magical realism as political mode
Mythology & comparative religion: Joseph Campbell's monomyth — hero's journey structure and critique
Vedic cosmology and the concept of Brahman
Norse cosmogony — Yggdrasil, Ragnarök, and cyclical time
Narrative theory: unreliable narrators — types and epistemological stakes
structuralism and Propp's morphology of the folktale
magical realism as a mode (not a genre)
the death of the author (Barthes) and reader-response theory
Cultural & intellectual history: Gutenberg's revolution and the standardization of knowledge
the Enlightenment salons and the public sphere (Habermas)
the Frankfurt School — Adorno, Horkheimer, and the culture industry
